data.title: Fubo introduces premium subscription without needing base channel plan
data.description:
• FuboTV (NYSE:FUBO) has launched a standalone premium subscription service for users to subscribe to select live and on-demand content without a base Fubo channel plan, the company said Tuesday.
  • FanDuel Sports Network, NBA League Pass, and Paramount+ with Showtime are the first networks offered to customers as standalone subscriptions, the company said.
  • The networks will continue to be available in Fubo’s Pro base plan (FanDuel Sports Network) or as add-on channel packages.
  • Subscribers of the standalone packages will also receive Fubo Free, a collection of nearly 200 free ad-supported streaming television channels.
  • Fubo said it now offers three different channel plan tiers: Fubo Free, Premium Subscription Services, and Virtual MVPD.
  • Customers who subscribe to live linear subscription services will also receive Unlimited Cloud DVR and the ability to stream from multiple screens simultaneously.
  • Launches of additional standalone premium subscription services, both live linear and SVOD, will be announced.
  • Press Release.

data.title: Invesco Q3 earnings beat by a penny as AUM hits new record high
data.description:
Invesco (NYSE:IVZ) stock gained 1.1% in Tuesday morning trading after its Q3 earnings slightly topped Wall Street expectations as the firm recorded a new all-time high in asset under management and garnered strong net inflows.

"Importantly, we again generated positive operating leverage, and continued improvement in our operating income and margin," said President and CEO Andrew Schlossberg. Our concentrated effort over the past several years to improve the balance sheet continues to pay off, putting us in position to repurchase $25 million of common stock during the quarter," he added.

Q3 adjusted EPS of $0.44, a hair above the $0.43 average analyst estimate, edged up from $0.43 in Q2 and $0.35 in Q3 2023.

Net revenue of $1.10B, trailing the $1.11B consensus, advanced 2.2% from the previous quarter and 5.1% from a year before.

Adjusted operating expenses increased to $755.5M from $750.5M in Q2 and retreated from $789.0M in Q3 2023.

Adjusted operating margin was 31.6% vs. 30.9% in Q2 and 28.2% in Q3 2023.

Ending AUM rose to $1.80T from $1.72T in Q2 and $1.49T in Q3 of last year.

Net long-term inflows of $16.5B at Sept. 30, mainly driven by ETFs and Index, Fundamental Fixed Income and Multi-Asset/Other asset classes, slipped from $16.7B at June 30 and surged from $2.6B at Sept. 30, 2023.

data.title: Genuine Parts Company shares slide on eroding profitability, lowered FY24 outlook
data.description:
Shares of Genuine Parts Company (NYSE:GPC) fell by a record 18% at Tuesday’s open as the company’s disappointing Q3 results coupled with significantly lowered guidance for FY24 chased shares to their lowest level since March 2022, making GPC the worst performing stock in the S&P 500 on Tuesday.

"Our results were below our expectations, primarily driven by continued weakness in market conditions in Europe and our Industrial business. While the external environment remains challenging for the balance of 2024, we expect the combination of near-term actions and long-term investments to better position us when market conditions improve,” CEO Will Stengel said.

The Atlanta-based parent company of NAPA Auto Parts earned a profit of $1.88 per share, down from $2.49 a year ago and $0.55 below market expectations, even as revenue increased by a modest 2.5%. The increase in sales was attributed to 3.2% benefit from acquisitions and a 0.1% favorable foreign exchange impact.

Comparable sales for its automotive segment increased 0.2%, while industrial comparable sales fell by 2.4%, resulting in total net comparable sales down 0.8% for the quarter. Overall sales in its global automotive business were up 4.8%, largely due to the acquisition of additional stores, while sales in its industrial parts business fell 1.2%.

Profit margins for both segments deteriorated, however, with automotive down 200 basis points to 6.9% and industrial down 100 basis points to 11.9%.

For the full year, the company now expects revenue growth of just 1% to 2% versus an earlier forecast of 1% to 3% growth, while earnings are now expected to be between $8.00 and $8.20 per share from initial guidance of $9.30 to $9.50 per share. This compares to the consensus estimate of $9.36.

While certain analysts may point to the outsized drop in the share price as a buying opportunity, this is now the second consecutive quarter the company has missed Wall Street’s expectations and shows a worrisome decay in profitability/margins over previous quarters.

data.title: Molecular Partners stock climbs 6% on expanded radiopharmaceuticals deal
data.description:
Molecular Partners (NASDAQ:MOLN) shares rose 6% on news it has expanded its agreement with radiopharmaceuticals company Orano Med for the development of Pb-based radio-DARPin therapeutics for the treatment of cancer.

Under the expanded agreement, Molecular will hold the commercialization rights MP0712 and a second nominated drug candidate, while Oran will hold the development and commercialization rights to the third and fourth candidates.

The companies hope to initiate first-in-human studies for MP0712 in 2025, pending regulatory clearance, according to a statement.

Molecular said it didn’t expect expansion of the agreement to have an immediate impact on its financial forecast for fiscal 2024. It is also maintaining its funding guidance into 2027.

The Swiss company said it had around CHF 140M in cash and equivalents as of the end of September.

data.title: U.S. Treasury extends 25% tax credit to solar wafer plants
data.description:
Solar wafer manufacturing projects in the U.S. will qualify for a 25% tax credit under a new rule that could unlock investment in domestic production of the equipment, Bloomberg reported Tuesday.

The new Treasury Department rule unexpectedly extends the reach of an investment tax credit created under the separate U.S. Chips and Science Act that is meant to nurture a local semiconductor supply chain.

Under the policy, both semiconductor and solar wafers will qualify for the credit, the Treasury Department announced.

The department said it adopted the expanded tax credit approach because of "specific supply chain and national security considerations regarding the production of solar wafers."

ETF: (NYSEARCA:TAN)

data.title: Lockheed Martin’s stock falls on disappointing sales growth
data.description:
Lockheed Martin’s (NYSE:LMT) shares fell as much as 4.6% in the first hour of regular trading on Tuesday after the aerospace and defense contractor reported disappointing sales growth.

Sales edged up 1% from a year earlier to $17.1 billion, less than the average estimate among Wall Street analysts for $17.37 billion.

Net earnings were $1.6 billion, or $6.80 a share, compared with $1.7 billion, or $6.73 a share, a year earlier.

Excluding one-time items, adjusted earnings were $6.84 a share, beating the consensus estimate of $6.44 a share.

Management raised its full-year earnings guidance after sales rose and its order backlog climbed to a record in the three-month period through September 29.

Revenue fell in the company’s aeronautics business, which makes the F-35 fighter jet, and in Lockheed’s (LMT) space unit.

Its missiles and fire-control business, which makes the HIMARS air-defense system, saw sales gains, as did the rotary and mission systems segment.

Lockheed’s (LMT) backlog rose to a record of more than $165 billion during the quarter with higher production in its missile programs.

Management lifted its 2024 earnings forecast to $26.65 a share from a previous range of $26.10 to $26.60 a share. It now expects $71.25 billion in annual revenue, narrowing its prior projection for $70.5 billion to $71.5 billion.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Tuesday: PM, GM, ZION, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower on Tuesday as cautious investor sentiment grew ahead of critical earnings reports from major companies.

Here are some of Tuesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • General Motors (GM) shares climbed over 5% after reporting strong Q3 results, driven by the strength of its internal combustion engine (ICE) business, which accounts for more than 80% of its U.S. operations. GM also raised its full-year 2024 profit outlook, now expecting a bottom-line profit of $14B to $15B, up from the previous forecast of $13B to $15B. Net income is forecasted between $10.4B and $11.1B, compared to the prior guidance of $10.0B to $11.4B, resulting in an adjusted EPS outlook of $10.00 to $10.50. Additionally, GM raised its adjusted automotive free cash flow forecast to $12.5B-$13.5B, up from $9.5B-$11.5B.
  • Zions Bancorporation (ZION) shares rose 4% after reporting Q3 results that exceeded expectations, highlighted by a 21% jump in profits. The net interest margin also strengthened to 3.03% from 2.93% a year earlier, reflecting improved operational performance.
  • Philip Morris (PM) shares soared 7% after delivering better-than-expected Q3 results, with record quarterly net revenues and profit. CEO Jacek Olczak highlighted strong performance across all regions and categories, driven by reaccelerated IQOS adjusted in-market sales growth, robust ZYN volumes, and resilient combustible product sales. Based on the strong year-to-date results, the company raised its full-year adjusted diluted EPS outlook to a range of $6.41-$6.51, surpassing the consensus of $6.40, up from the previous range of $6.33-$6.45.
  • iRhythm Technologies (IRTC) shares surged 18% after the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) granted clearance for its 510(k) submission concerning design changes made to the Zio AT device. The Zio AT remains available for shipment to customers in the U.S. Quentin Blackford, iRhythm's President and CEO, emphasized that this clearance reflects the company's commitment to quality, compliance, and performance. He noted that this marks the first of two 510(k) clearances submitted earlier this year, both tied to ongoing remediation efforts with the FDA. The company remains dedicated to ensuring patient safety and maintaining physician trust in the clinical performance of the Zio AT system.

Biggest stock losers

  • DMC Global (BOOM) shares plummeted 17% after lowering Q3 sales guidance to $152M from its previous outlook of $158M-$168M. Weaker-than-expected sales at its Arcadia Products and DynaEnergetics businesses contributed to the decline. Q3 adjusted EBITDA is now expected to be $5M, down from the previous estimate of $15M-$18M. DMC also announced a $142M non-cash goodwill impairment charge associated with its 2021 acquisition of Arcadia. The board has decided to pause marketing efforts for DynaEnergetics and NobelClad, prioritizing stability amid recent macroeconomic challenges in the energy market.
  • NANO Nuclear Energy (NNE) shares fell over 7% after the company filed a prospectus for the offer and sale of approximately 1.4M units, consisting of 1.4M shares of common stock and warrants to purchase up to 699,301 additional shares of common stock.
  • Verizon Communications (VZ) shares declined 5% after reporting mixed Q3 results, including a 40% drop in retail postpaid net additions to 349,000. Despite the weaker performance in postpaid additions, Verizon reaffirmed its FY2024 outlook, expecting wireless service revenue growth of 2.0%-3.5% and adjusted EBITDA growth of 1.0%-3.0%. The company also maintained its adjusted EPS guidance of $4.50-$4.70 (midpoint of $4.60), compared to the $4.57 consensus, with capital expenditures projected between $17.0B and $17.5B. The adjusted effective income tax rate is anticipated to be in the range of 22.5% to 24.0%.
  • GE Aerospace (GE) shares plummeted over 7% after the company missed top-line consensus in Q3, despite a 28% rise in total orders to $12.6B. Commercial engines and services orders grew 29% Y/Y, with services and equipment both increasing over 20%. Defense orders were up 19% in the same period. While management maintained its 2024 adjusted revenue forecast of high single-digit growth, it raised its operating profit guidance to $6.7B-$6.9B from the prior range of $6.5B-$6.8B. GE also revised its adjusted EPS forecast to $4.20-$4.35, up from $3.95-$4.20, and increased its free cash flow outlook to $5.6B-$5.8B. Commercial engines and services revenue is expected to grow in the low-double digits to mid-teens, while defense and propulsion technology revenue is projected to rise in the mid- to high-single digits.

data.title: Richmond Fed Manufacturing Index records another weak print in October
data.description: • The Richmond Fed Manufacturing Index improved to -14 in October from -21 in September, compared with the -19 consensus.

data.title: J.M. Smucker divests the Voortman brand as part of a portfolio optimization plan
data.description:
J.M. Smucker Co. (NYSE:SJM) announced on Tuesday that it entered into a definitive agreement to sell its Voortman cookie brand to Second Nature Brands, which is a U.S.-based creator of premium snacks and treats controlled by CapVest Partners. The all-cash transaction was valued at approximately $305 million.

The food giant highlighted that the decision reflects the company's continued commitment to optimize its portfolio and reallocate resources to its core growth brands. The transaction includes all Voortman trademarks and the company's leased manufacturing facility in Burlington, Ontario, Canada.

Notably, the Voortman brand generated net sales of approximately $65 million for the company's fiscal year ended April 30, which represents a partial year of net sales reported in its Sweet Baked Snacks segment results following its acquisition on November 7, 2023. For fiscal year 2025, the company anticipates full-year net sales from the Voortman brand to be approximately $150 million.

CEO statement: "This decision reflects our continued commitment to portfolio and resource optimization to focus on our largest growth opportunities as a Company... the divestiture of the Voortman brand is an important step in our integration plans that will enable the execution of our Sweet Baked Snacks strategy through dedicated focus and ongoing investments in the Hostess brand, advancing our leadership in the sweet baked goods category."

In addition to the Voortman announcement, J.M. Smucker (SJM) also confirmed continued progress on the integration of Hostess Brands, including the successful completion of systems integration earlier this month, and that it remains on track to achieve expected synergies of $100 million from the acquisition by the end of fiscal year 2026.
